item.title: Farmers in WA food bowl region take $25 million cyclone hit
item.description: Growers across Western Australia's Gascoyne take a huge financial hit after four natural disasters in the first four months of 2026.

item.title: This easy 'hug in a bowl' chicken stew is perfect for midweek dinners - ABC News
item.description: "It's a shortcut to that feeling that you get when you've slow cooked something all Sunday, but without that time or labour."

item.title: Cox's goal celebration sparks scuffle as Freo holds off Carlton comeback
item.description: Mason Cox gets in the face of Ben Ainsworth after kicking a fourth-quarter goal, resulting in some fireworks as Fremantle wins its sixth consecutive match.

item.title: Three men arrested after alleged arson attack in Melbourne CBD
item.description: Police were led on a foot-chase by alleged arsonists after a bar was set alight in ACDC lane early on Saturday morning.

item.title: Cotton industry 'alert but not alarmed' by increasing numbers of bicoloured weevils in NT crops
item.description: Farmers and researchers notice a rise in bicoloured weevils feeding on cotton crops in the Northern Territory.

item.title: SA Police say person whose body was found at Inman Valley was likely murdered
item.description: SA Police say initial investigations suggest a person whose body has been found in bushland south of Adelaide was murdered.

item.title: NSW childcare worker charged with allegedly slapping two boys in Sydney's south
item.description: A 25-year-old man was arrested at an address in Belmore on Saturday morning.

item.title: How Henry the dachshund puppy survived three months lost in Perth's southern suburbs
item.description: A dachshund puppy is reunited with its owner after being lost for almost three months, in an adventure spanning multiple suburbs.

item.title: Captain Grant bristles at 'silly question' as Storm sink to historic low
item.description: Melbourne captain Harry Grant has defended coach Craig Bellamy after the Storm sank to a historic low following a 48-6 belting at the hands of South Sydney.

item.title: Beaches at Sydney's Royal National Park close after dead whale brings 'significant' shark activity
item.description: Several popular beaches in Sydney's Royal National Park are closed due to increased shark activity after a large whale carcass washes up on a rock platform.

item.title: Black Ferns outclass Wallaroos in Anzac Day Test
item.description: The Black Ferns produce a dominant performance to defeat the Wallaroos 40-5 in the first Anzac Day Test between the trans-Tasman rivals.

item.title: Donald Trump cancels Pakistan trip for US envoys as Middle East peace hopes fade
item.description: Donald Trump cancels a trip to Pakistan by US representatives Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, with talks aimed at ending the two-month Iran war at a standstill as both Tehran and Washington show little willingness to soften their terms.

item.title: Today in history, April 26: Chernobyl's nuclear disaster, 40 years on
item.description: Nuclear meltdown in 1986 reshaped ecosystems and human lives. Four decades on the legacy of the Chernobyl disaster is still unfolding.

item.title: Ringo Starr brought country music to The Beatles — now he's getting back to it
item.description: Ringo's childhood love of classic country still influences the new music that The Beatles's drummer makes.

item.title: Calling all word wizards. Guy Montgomery's here with your Sunday spelling quiz
item.description: Delighted by diagraphs or hyped for homophones? Step inside and showcase your spelling skills.

item.title: Booing at Anzac Day services shows lack of public awareness of racism in Australia
item.description: Jeers could be heard for more than a minute as Uncle Ray Minniecon spoke at yesterday's Anzac service in Sydney with similar scenes playing out in Melbourne and Perth.

item.title: Sex workers speak out about the HIV crisis in Papua New Guinea
item.description: Workers have told the ABC about the battle to stay safe as the Pacific nation battles a rising number of infections.

item.title: Former Treasury boss says Australians left with little to show from commodities boom
item.description: Former Treasury secretary Ken Henry says the under-taxed resources sector has hollowed out Australia's economy, and raising more revenue from the sale of LNG resources was in the national interest.

item.title: NDIS cuts show when Labor will move, gas tax shows when it won't
item.description: Labor's radical transformation of the NDIS as it resists a growing chorus of support for a new gas tax exposes the limit of its willingness to act when given a social licence.

item.title: Did you know Australia has three flags? Red, white and blue
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Gippsland footballer with Guillain-Barré syndrome returns to field after induced coma
item.description: A Gippsland footballer who spent 15 days in an induced coma after contracting a rare nerve condition has made a remarkable return to the field.

item.title: Long-held bitumen standards waived after supply caught in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Drivers could soon be travelling on roads built for US and European climates, as supply disruptions force governments to change the standard of bitumen used in Australia.

item.title: Dragons halfback Kade Reed to keep starting role, says interim coach Dean Young
item.description: Dean Young backs Kade Reed to remain St George Illawarra's halfback even after the Dragons slumped to a 62-16 thrashing in the 20-year-old's highly anticipated NRL debut.

item.title: Victoria's Moorabool River is struggling for water, but there is still life in it yet
item.description: A small fish that smells like freshly-cut cucumber could be akin to a canary in the coalmine for one of Victoria's most stressed rivers.

item.title: What happens to a car once it has been stolen and wrecked, and what if hazards are left inside?
item.description: 'Opportunistic' car thieves are leaving vehicles littered with syringes, weapons and even traps, and in 2025, 77 per cent of vehicles inspected by one contractor had signs of methylamphetamine used at levels harmful to health.

item.title: 'Tombstone tourists' keeping Australia's oldest surviving European cemetery alive in Parramatta
item.description: Nestled between apartment blocks and opposite an escape room, St John's Cemetery holds many unique stories from history.

item.title: Ollie Hollands a late out for Carlton after Elijah Hollands's struggles, but coach Michael Voss says he is 'in a good space'
item.description: Carlton coach Michael Voss says Elijah Hollands's younger brother Ollie is doing OK despite being a late out for the club's Anzac Day clash with Fremantle.

item.title: Predator-free farm helps endangered species thrive
item.description: A predator-proof fence at a Victorian farm is helping endangered species recover while proving conservation and agriculture can successfully co-exist.

item.title: Queensland's remote national park islands where you can volunteer and holiday for free
item.description: North West Island is one of five national parks where you can holiday in exchange for campground hosting duties.

item.title: Ancient, critically endangered 'sterile' plant King's lomatia found for second time in Tasmanian wilderness
item.description: A second population of an ancient tree, King's lomatia, has been found in the south-west of Tasmania. A scientist says the precise location must be kept secret, or risk being 'killed by love'.

item.title: Thirty years after losing his family in the Port Arthur massacre, Walter Mikac says gun control is still vital
item.description: On April 28, 1996, a gunman killed 35 people at Port Arthur. Walter Mikac's wife and two daughters were among them. Thirty years on, he says gun control is as important as ever.

item.title: Indigenous kids training for life in and out of the ring at youth development camp in Canberra
item.description: A Canberra Indigenous-led boxing camp is helping young fighters hone their skills for life inside and outside the ring in its first development camp, continuing a proud tradition of First Nations involvement in the sport.

item.title: Seed swaps, preserved food and community gardens as preppers brace for uncertain future
item.description: It may not be the end of the world, but these Australians are finding creative ways to beat the cost-of-living crisis and improve their food security in troubled times.

item.title: Preparing food for future use with a pressure cooker
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Indonesian farmers improve food security while protecting Australia from citrus disease
item.description: An Australian-funded project is educating Indonesian farmers about a devastating citrus disease that has spread to all major growing regions except Australia.

item.title: Police investigate suspected drive-by shooting, firebombing in Melbourne's south-east
item.description: Police are investigating multiple incidents overnight including a drive-by shooting, suspected firebombing and an allegedly violent attack on a tram.

item.title: Why Shaun Micallef wrote 'gothic romance novel' De'Ath Takes a Holiday
item.description: After Shaun Micallef finished up 11 years hosting comedy news show Mad as Hell in 2022, the comedian did something unexpected: he wrote a 'gothic romance novel'.

item.title: Melbourne on track to record its longest run of warm April days in more than a decade
item.description: A blocking high has put Melbourne on track for its longest run of warm April days in more than a decade.

item.title: Catalpa captain George Anthony's descendants share historic family heirlooms with Fremantle Prison
item.description: Captain George Anthony's family share historic documents detailing the life of the principled Quaker who was branded a pirate for his role in the Catalpa rescue.

item.title: Married couple retire in their 90s after running small town's only hardware store for decades
item.description: The Pickles have been running the only hardware store in Junee for six decades, but now they are handing over the keys.

item.title: Angus Taylor avoids backing ASIO amid moves by ISIS-linked families to return to Australia
item.description: Government action needs to be taken to prevent a group of Australian families with links to former ISIS fighters from re-entering the country, Opposition Leader Angus Taylor says.

item.title: Resources industry in the spotlight during Angus Taylor's first trip to WA as Opposition leader
item.description: This past week marks the first time the Liberal leader visited WA since he took the position, and during his trip west, both Taylor and Premier Cook were navigating the state's hot-button issues.

item.title: Donald Trump evacuated from White House correspondents' dinner after suspected shooter apprehended
item.description: US President Donald Trump is evacuated from the White House Correspondents' Dinner after a security incident.

item.title: 'Main offender' dingo euthanised after multiple attacks in Karijini National Park
item.description: A male dingo has been "humanely destroyed" after multiple attacks within days of each other at Western Australia's famous Karijini National Park.

item.title: Mother of woman who died at Bandyup Women's Prison questions if daughter had adequate care
item.description: Deaths in custody are on the rise and family members and advocates for a woman who died in a Perth prison say more needs to be done to keep inmates safe.

item.title: Armed police scramble during security scare at White House press dinner
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Love story: Bonding with my daughter was amazing, but it took time
item.description: Perinatal depression and anxiety meant I thought my daughter would be better off with a different mum. But with the right support, things got better.

item.title: Only one known single spyridium plant is left in the wild on the Coorong, but scientists are hunting for more
item.description: Only one known single spyridium plant is left in the wild, in crushed limestone on the side of a road. But scientists are hunting for more.

item.title: US attorney for DC speaks after incident at White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Journalist’s firsthand account of Trump's evacuation from correspondents’ dinner
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Dawson fights back tears after return as GWS edge Roos amid ARC drama
item.description: Adelaide skipper Jordan Dawson breaks down in tears after playing his first match since his brother's tragic death, as GWS prevails against North with the aid of a major ARC blunder.

item.title: Tasmanian coastal community of Dolphin Sands on bushfire watch again
item.description: A coastal community recently ravaged by a bushfire has again been on alert in Tasmania.

item.title: Trump posts security vision of suspect from correspondents’ dinner
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Manly's revival continues as Foran sets record, Panthers down Knights
item.description: Manly's revival under interim coach Kieran Foran has continued with a fourth straight win, as the Sea Eagles scored five tries in the second half to sink the Parramatta Eels.

item.title: US attorney for DC details charges laid against suspect
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Sydney beaches to remain closed for days due to decomposing whale carcass attracting sharks
item.description: Popular beaches along Sydney's Royal National Park will remain closed for days, as authorities face the immense task of moving a large whale carcass that is attracting sharks.

item.title: Qld Premier David Crisafulli attends Anzac Day event with no Acknowledgement of Country
item.description: The event in Townsville, which has a proud military history and is Australia's largest garrison city, also did not include a Welcome to Country.

item.title: ABC’s John Lyons inside the White House correspondent dinner
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Teenager in critical condition after alleged stabbing on bus in Perth's north
item.description: A 16-year-old boy has been charged for allegedly stabbing another teen, also 16, during a fight on a bus in Joondalup.

item.title: When Trump was evacuated from Washington dinner, we had no idea what was happening
item.description: The ABC's Americas editor was inside the ballroom when a shooting suspect was stopped by the Secret Service.

item.title: Man in custody after allegedly assaulting several people including nurses at Randwick's Prince of Wales Hospital
item.description: A 51-year-old man seriously injured a nurse's leg at Prince of Wales Hospital in Randwick about 8:30pm on Saturday night, before assaulting two other nurses and a security guard.

item.title: With yet another Trump security threat, questions mount for US authorities about keeping politicians safe
item.description: Questions are already being asked about how a man, allegedly carrying two guns and multiple knives, could have made it as far as he did.

item.title: Veteran's advice after Anzac Day booing: Find your 'warrior spirit'
item.description: While condemnation has been swift after Indigenous elders were booed on Anzac Day, solutions to this type of racism seem harder to pin down. Elders have advice to share.

item.title: Australia to sell fleet of Bushmasters to Netherlands, locks in commitment to build 268 more for itself
item.description: Australian Bushmaster armoured vehicles are being sent to the Netherlands following a request to Australia by the Dutch government.

item.title: Sixteen dead in strikes as Ukraine marks 40 years since Chernobyl
item.description: Marking the anniversary, Volodymyr Zelenskyy warns that Russia is using "nuclear terrorism" as a weapon of war.

item.title: This week in history quiz, April 27: Have you got the memory to beat our quiz?
item.description: Which Australian city played host to Expo 88? And what age did the world's oldest spider reach?

item.title: Daniel MacPherson and Amy Shark fight for their lives in Beast
item.description: Bosch & Rockit filmmaker Tyler Atkins's latest feature put his stars through the wringer on camera, but held them with love behind the scenes.

item.title: Budget is in the dark as Hormuz oil crisis upends economic outlook
item.description: Accompanying the oil shock unfolding in the Strait of Hormuz is a profound change in the way fiscal and monetary policymakers operate. And as the government prepares the budget with the impact of the Iran war still unfolding, a recession may be ahead.

item.title: Why women are getting less sleep than men — and what we can do about it
item.description: There are biological and psychological reasons behind why women experience more disrupted sleep than men, but experts say the mental load is another powerful yet less obvious contributor.

item.title: Trump is political kryptonite in Australia but diplomacy still guides the way
item.description: As Donald Trump's approval in Australia sinks, Anthony Albanese needs to walk a careful line: keep voters convinced of the merits of the alliance, while avoiding the impression he is being too reverential.

item.title: Donald Trump was 'all set to really rip' into the media, but shooting may help change his tune
item.description: While the latest act of violence wasn't as close as Trump's brush with death in Butler, Pennsylvania, almost two years ago, it will no doubt revive some trauma for Trump and those closest to him.

item.title: Japan is sending warships to Australia in a landmark deal, and there could be plenty more to follow
item.description: Australia's multi-billion-dollar deal with Japan for its future warships could set a template that will help Tokyo rapidly expand defence exports across Asia.

item.title: Suspect due to face court over Trump press dinner incident as security measures probed
item.description: The suspected gunman arrested at the White House correspondents' dinner on the weekend is expected to soon be charged in federal court.

item.title: China's 'quirky' fix for property crisis rewards marathon runners, AI experts and tennis players
item.description: Local governments in China are offering marathon runners, AI experts and tennis players subsidies to purchase unwanted properties amid a lingering real estate downturn.

item.title: The 'groundbreaking' AI tool helping Victorian rangers protect native species in a fraction of the time
item.description: A new AI tool will help Parks Victoria manage native and endangered species affected by natural disasters, including the summer bushfires, with the department making the technology available to everyone.

item.title: Why street artist Peter Drew decided to bring back his 'Aussie' poster series
item.description: Peter Drew has been hanging his controversial posters around Australia for 10 years, and has no plans to stop.

item.title: Queensland Police Service launches road safety operation after enforcement drop post COVID-19
item.description: The ABC last year revealed a sharp drop in road safety enforcement hours from police in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic.

item.title: Subdivisions taking place in Adelaide's 'visionary' Hills Face Zone
item.description: The Hills Face Zone was legislated to protect the natural character of Adelaide's landscape backdrop but, despite subdivisions not being permitted, there have been 11 over the past decade.

item.title: Victorian budget's $3.5 million to fund security upgrades for family violence victim-survivors
item.description: The Victorian government has announced women facing the danger of family violence will be able to upgrade their home security under a $3.5 million pledge.

item.title: Platypuses need threatened species recognition in NSW, researcher says
item.description: A leading researcher is calling for the platypus to be recognised as a threatened species in NSW, saying a lack of data is the only reason it has not been reclassified.

item.title: Caitlin Foord shines as Arsenal win Champions League semi-final first leg lead, Sam Kerr on target for Chelsea in WSL
item.description: Arsenal's Matildas are scenting more Champions League glory after the holders beat OL Lyonnes 2-1 in the first leg of their semi-final at the Emirates Stadium, while Matildas captain Sam Kerr scored twice to keep Chelsea's slim WSL title ambitions alive in a 4-1 win over Everton.

item.title: From gangsters' paradise to police sting. How Istanbul turned on Australia's fugitives
item.description: Istanbul was once the hideout of choice for Australian crooks. Now a Sydney gangster's legal challenge has revealed how it all unravelled for the "Aussie Cartel".

item.title: Fuel costs and supply worries make for 'slow' Easter in regional South Australia
item.description: Fuel costs force down tourism numbers in some typically busy South Australian towns across Easter and the school holidays.

item.title: AI decision review system being trialled in Darwin women's division one cricket competition
item.description: Darwin's premier women's cricket league becomes the first Australian grade competition to use an artificial intelligence umpire review system.

item.title: Wet week ahead for south-east Qld due to unusually 'persistent' weather system
item.description: The "very slow-moving" high-pressure system causing the wet weather should "finally get a move on" early next week, the BOM says.

item.title: Fuel prices create anxiety for Queensland's community events, but the show must go on
item.description: The nation's fuel crisis continues to hamper community events across regional Queensland, as organisers grapple with soaring costs.

item.title: Canberra is growing its economic 'pie' to become more than a city of public administration
item.description: The capital has long been known as a public service town, but with about two in three workers now employed in the private sector and the population expected to reach nearly 700,000 by 2050, is that reputation — and key economic driver — likely to change?

item.title: Frustration with war effort sparks renewed push to bring down Netanyahu — as it happened
item.description: Former Israeli prime ministers Naftali Bennett and Yair Lapid are merging their two parties into a new entity called "Together" in a bid to defeat Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu at the next election.

item.title: Esteban Andrada punches Jorge Pulido, sparking Aragonese derby brawl between Real Zaragoza and Huesca
item.description: The Aragonese derby between Real Zaragoza and Huesca descends into a wild brawl after Zaragoza goalkeeper Esteban Andrada punches Huesca skipper Jorge Pulido in the face.

item.title: Two dead in house fire on day family were due to move interstate
item.description: Two bodies found in a house that was extensively damaged by fire in the foothills of the NSW Blue Mountains are believed to be those of two missing children.

item.title: Cameron Green hits form at right time as Kolkata Knight Riders beat Lucknow Super Giants in extraordinary IPL clash
item.description: It's taken a while but Cameron Green is showing why he's worth the big pay packet as he helped Kolkata win an astonishing IPL Super Over affair against Lucknow.

item.title: How four remote Queensland farms became the oil capital of Australia
item.description: With oil exploration back in the headlines, here's how Australia struck it lucky in the 1960s.

item.title: The history of the Moonie Oil Fields in Queensland
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Influencers to pay $100 to make money off Tasmania's national parks under new permit system
item.description: Influencers and content creators will be able to film and profit from content gathered in Tasmania's national parks for just $100, under new regulations.

item.title: Nelly Korda wins The Chevron Championship, her third major, marking a return to world number one
item.description: Nelly Korda claimed her third major title, winning The Chevron Championship at Memorial Park by a crushing five shots.

item.title: Rare Queensland set of bells rings its first peal in three-hour 'marathon' effort
item.description: Australia's heaviest set of six bells completes its historic first peal — a mathematical order change of 5,040 rings across three hours.

item.title: Mother calls for a more accessible world after daughter's cerebral palsy diagnosis
item.description: Ayla doesn't let disability slow her down, but her mother wants her to have the same opportunities as others.

item.title: Bad Company: Art and big business collide in new Anne Edmonds, Kitty Flanagan comedy
item.description: Everything Anne Edmonds has done over the last two decades has been leading to Bad Company, an unfettered satire of the theatre world the comedian created, wrote and stars in alongside Kitty Flanagan.

item.title: McDonald's to set up Northcote store after overturning council ruling
item.description: The fast food giant has been cleared to set up an outlet in the Melbourne suburb of Northcote after successfully challenging a council decision to block its restaurant plans.

item.title: WA Criminal Mental Impairment Act 'impossible' to fully implement in practice, judge says
item.description: WA legal experts say the Criminal Law Mental Impairment Act isn't working as intended, citing a case in Broome where a man was found not criminally responsible but still jailed.

item.title: Japan's north struck by magnitude-6.1 earthquake, followed by wildfires prompting evacuations
item.description: A preliminary magnitude-6.1 earthquake struck northern Japan early today, the US Geological Survey has reported, but there are no immediate reports of fatalities or damage.

item.title: Sabastian Sawe ran the marathon in an 'unbelievable time'. Here's why we can believe in it
item.description: Sabastian Sawe smashes one of athletics' seemingly unbreakable barriers by running a marathon faster than two hours. Some call it unbelievable, but the Kenyan runner has done everything he can to ensure it stands the test of time.

item.title: Sheep farmers swear by Lifetime Ewe course to steer them through drought and diesel price spikes
item.description: With a quick feel of a sheep's back, farmers score their flock, finding the magic numbers to help them navigate record-breaking drought, and now diesel price spikes.

item.title: NT Police seek 47-year-old Jefferson Lewis in relation to missing 5-year-old Alice Springs girl
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Police search waterways in Adelaide's south for knives linked to alleged murder of Caleb Leriano
item.description: SA Police will search waterways in Adelaide's south today to find three knives they believe are connected to the alleged murder of 24-year-old Caleb Leriano.

item.title: Australian Gregory Louden on leading PlayStation's next big title, Saros
item.description: Developer Gregory Louden always wanted to work in games, but it was a sidestep into filmmaking that helped him secure his dream job at Finnish studio Housemarque.

item.title: Who is Cole Tomas Allen, Donald Trump's would-be assassin?
item.description: Details from the life of US President Donald Trump's would-be assassin paint a picture of a high achiever who took an abrupt turn towards political violence.

item.title: Orthopaedic surgeon Herwig Drobetz returns to Gaza for fourth mercy mission
item.description: Orthopaedic surgeon Herwig Drobetz returns to Gaza for fourth mercy mission.

item.title: Kerry Kennedy thought of father's assassination when shooter rushed security at gala dinner
item.description: Human rights activist Kerry Kennedy praises a "superhero" member of Congress for shielding her when an active shooter attempted to storm the annual White House Correspondents' Association dinner.

item.title: Here are the candidates in Tasmania's Legislative Council elections in Huon and Rosevears
item.description: Voters in the Legislative Council divisions of Huon and Rosevears will head to the polls on Saturday. The outcome of the elections could determine the future of the greyhound industry in Tasmania.

item.title: Victorian government promises $1 billion in budget to fix state's roads
item.description: The funding package also includes nearly $37 million dedicated to cleaning up graffiti along major roads.

item.title: WA's far north 'blindsided' by Boskalis's marine sand proposal and lack of consultation
item.description: A dredging company acknowledges concerns from some community members in northern Western Australia that public meetings should have been held earlier.

item.title: PNG prime minister encouraged by Jarome Luai's visit and meeting with Chiefs bosses
item.description: The Wests Tigers playmaker is the Chiefs' top target ahead of their NRL entry in 2028, and the Papua New Guinea prime minister says his trip to PNG shows the interest is mutual.

item.title: Indigenous veteran Di Ryder slams 'distracting' booing at Anzac Day Welcome to Country
item.description: The Whadjuk Noongar elder who was booed while giving a Welcome to Country at Perth's Anzac Day dawn service says she had a right to speak at the event and will not back down.

item.title: Tadej Pogačar's latest rival reveals himself as Paul Seixas challenges champion at Liège-Bastogne-Liège
item.description: Tadej Pogačar claims a third monument win out of four for the year at Liège-Bastogne-Liège, but a new power is rising in the form of 19-year-old Paul Seixas, with an unlikely Tour de France tilt seemingly on the cards.

item.title: Gormandale footballers break 65-game losing streak with victory in North Gippsland league
item.description: Gormandale players are over the moon after breaking a 65-game losing streak on the weekend.

item.title: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas says algal bloom 'gone' but fishing restrictions need to expand
item.description: Fishing restrictions will be strengthened in South Australia due to the algal bloom's devastating impact on stocks, but the government says the bloom itself has "essentially cleared up".

item.title: Four easy egg recipes for any occasion
item.description: If you're looking to add the humble egg into your weekly meals, we have you covered.

item.title: AFL great Mick Malthouse struck alleged intruder in home raid, court hears
item.description: Legendary AFL coach Mick Malthouse "swung an exercise pole" and hit an intruder in the head as he defended his family during an alleged attempted home invasion last year, a Melbourne court has heard.

item.title: Entertainer Oz Pearlman recounts moment of White House dinner shooting
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Paralympic swimmer Alexa Leary calls for traumatic brain injury awareness day
item.description: Alexa Leary sustained a traumatic brain injury (TBI) after a cycling accident at age 19. The two-time Paralympic gold medallist turned author is calling for a day to recognise TBI and the unique challenges it brings.

item.title: RSL president on making Welcome to Country 'more tailored' to Anzac Day
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Snowfall in April, record hot day in May. Is this a new norm for Tasmania? Scientists explain
item.description: Tasmania is well into autumn, but firefighters remain on alert as late-season bushfires have threatened communities. This Friday marks the first day of May, and with it could come one of the state's hottest May days in nearly 140 years of record-keeping.

item.title: Prediction markets accused of insider trading and profiting from state secrets
item.description: A US soldier accused of using classified information to place bets on a covert military operation has thrown a spotlight on the fast-growing world of prediction markets.

item.title: RSL Australia to review Welcome to Country policies after elders booed on Anzac Day
item.description: RSL Australia will review its Welcome to Country guidelines after Indigenous elders were booed during Anzac Day services across the country.

item.title: AFL concedes error on controversial ARC call as league boss says umpiring 'as good as it's been for a long time'
item.description: AFL football boss Greg Swann says most of the feedback he receives from the clubs surrounding the officiating in the league is positive despite a pair of controversial review calls in Round 7.

item.title: Thomas Clarke sentenced to 21 years in prison for gruesome Dimboola murder
item.description: Thomas Clarke is sentenced to 21 years in prison for murdering a man in Dimboola in 2023.

item.title: Ex-wife of motorcyclist killed in Yarck hit and run says she hoped to reunite with him
item.description: The County Court hears emotional statements from the ex-wife and daughters of a motorcyclist who was killed by a drug-affected driver in a stolen car in Central Victoria.

item.title: Murder trial hears multiple theories surrounding Gavin Preston's death
item.description: Victoria's Supreme Court hears police received numerous tip-offs after underworld figure Gavin Preston was shot dead, including claims of a cemetery desecration and a feud with an illegal tobacco kingpin.

item.title: Monday Finance with Daniel Ziffer
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Taroom Trough's oil field touted as solution to fuel security but no-one knows how much oil is there
item.description: According to Queensland's premier the Taroom Trough could be the solution to averting a future fuel crisis and a once-in-a-generation opportunity to claw back sovereign capability.

item.title: CFMEU administrator Mark Irving KC resigns
item.description: Mark Irving KC says the next "rebuild phase" of the union requires someone with "a different set of skills".

item.title: Australia's minimum fuel reserves would double under $800m Coalition plan, at a cost to drivers
item.description: Australia would have 60 days' worth of fuel supplies in reserve under an $800 million Coalition proposal that would cost drivers one cent per litre.

item.title: Lebanon and Hezbollah at odds over peace talks with Israel as strikes continue despite ceasefire
item.description: Lebanon's president has accused Hezbollah of hypocrisy, as the Iranian-backed militant group demanded the government walk away from peace talks with Israel.

item.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman's court battle to reveal ongoing power struggle at Open AI
item.description: The world's richest man is accusing the company and its chief executive Sam Altman, and Microsoft of betraying the AI start up's original mission.

item.title: Press gala shooting suspect charged with attempted assassination of Donald Trump — as it happened
item.description: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, is charged with the attempted assassination of the president, a crime that carries a maximum sentence of life in prison. Take a look back at how the day's events unfolded.

item.title: Train collision near Jakarta kills 14 as rescuers complete evacuation
item.description: The train operator says the crash, on the outskirts of Jakarta, involved a commuter train and a long-distance train.

item.title: The best ways to protect credit and debit cards from fraud
item.description: Experts reveal the most common ways fraudsters steal card details and how to keep your accounts safer.

item.title: The moment Andy Lee feared exploits with Hamish Blake had gone too far
item.description: Andy Lee opens up on his 23-year working relationship with his "fast and loose" comedy partner Hamish Blake, and the stunt that left him fearing for his mate's life.

item.title: Girl's suspected abductor had been out of jail for just six days, police say
item.description: Northern Territory Police believe five-year-old Sharon Granites was abducted from a home in an Alice Springs town camp as the search continues into its third day.

item.title: The security breach that exposed Donald Trump to a shooting incident
item.description: A shooting incident at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has prompted a swirl of questions about how an alleged shooter breached Secret Service security and got so close to the US president.

item.title: Apple's Tim Cook another casualty as worries about AI's impact on jobs grow
item.description: Tim Cook helped usher in a communications revolution and his departure from Apple marks a turning point for the company. It also serves as a reminder of how AI is changing the tech landscape.

item.title: Health insurer Bupa accused of anti-competitive behaviour under secretive No Gap scheme deals
item.description: Leaked contracts obtained by the ABC reveal Bupa's deals with private hospitals impose stifling conditions on operators that are too small to push back.

item.title: Soaring petrol prices force tourism operators to introduce surcharges
item.description: Fuel costs have more than doubled for one of the Great Barrier Reef's major tourism operators — and it's forcing a rethink on how paradise is powered.

item.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?
item.description: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

item.title: Learn why tomorrow's inflation data could be important for you in just three minutes
item.description: There are some key moments coming up for the Australian economy and they could make a big difference to your bank account. Emily Stewart — aka Sensible Emily — is here to help you understand what to look out for.

item.title: Penny Wong will have to navigate China, Japan and South Korea tensions
item.description: When Foreign Minister Penny Wong travels to Japan, China and South Korea this week, she will move through a region where relationships point in different directions at the same time.

item.title: Saudi Arabia's dazzling vision was crashing into reality even before the Iran war broke out
item.description: Saudi Arabia's plans to transform its economy were already facing headwinds when the Iran war broke out, placing them even further in jeopardy.

item.title: Melbourne eating institution Marios celebrates 40th anniversary with free publication
item.description: It may have once denied Jerry Seinfield a booking, but this Fitzroy cafe has won no shortage of fans during its four decades.

item.title: Granddaughter of Port Arthur massacre's first victims speaks on 30th anniversary
item.description: Seascape guesthouse owners Sally and David Martin were the first people murdered at Port Arthur in 1996. On the 30th anniversary of the tragedy, their granddaughter tells of how the family has refused to be defined by it.

item.title: Australian rugby may benefit from copying Crusaders' lateral thinking with Leicester Fainga'anuku
item.description: Thinking outside the square has previously served Australian rugby well, which is why there are lessons it can take away from at least one of New Zealand's teams in Super Rugby Pacific.

item.title: Scenic Rim community divided over proposed Cleanaway waste-to-energy incinerator
item.description: A proposed waste-to-energy incinerator near Beaudesert divides the Scenic Rim community. Supporters say it could ease Queensland's landfill crisis, while opponents fear health and environmental risks.

item.title: Josh Hazlewood delivers IPL masterclass as Royal Challengers Bengaluru smash Delhi Capitals
item.description: Australian pace maestro Josh Hazlewood delivers his best performance of the IPL season, taking four wickets to spearhead another RCB win.

item.title: Farmer rescues kangaroo from mud in dry dam as drought conditions hit western NSW
item.description: Photos show a kangaroo moments before being rescued from a dam in Central West NSW, after it became bogged in mud while searching for water in an increasingly barren landscape.

item.title: Doctors concerned about $3.2b new Women’s and Children’s Hospital design
item.description: Doctors say parts of the proposed design for Adelaide's $3.2 billion new Women's and Children's Hospital raise "significant concerns", including the location of the women's assessment service.

item.title: Australians living in fear as calls grow for inquiry into foreign government harassment
item.description: Iranian Australian journalist and academic Saba Vasefi has spent years documenting human rights abuses in her homeland. The regime she's criticised has not forgotten her name.

item.title: New York mayor announces free FIFA World Cup fan zones as costs continue to escalate
item.description: New York City will host free events for soccer fans who can't afford the hefty ticket prices for the FIFA World Cup, Mayor Zohran Mamdani says.

item.title: Push to make voluntary assisted dying available through telehealth
item.description: Despite voluntary assisted dying being legal in Victoria, many regional patients are not getting the same end-of-life care.

item.title: CCTV to be installed on Gold Coast waterways to crack down on illegal boaties
item.description: The Gold Coast Waterways Authority says the initiative comes after more than 400 unregistered and unseaworthy vessels were removed from the city's waterways over the past five years.

item.title: Live: ASX loses ground, Bank of Japan keeps rates at 0.75pc
item.description: Australia's share market has lost ground ahead of Wednesday's inflation data. The Bank of Japan has kept the overnight rate at 0.75pc. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: NFL kicker Younghoe Koo's comical error revealed a brain tumour in fan Mark Toothaker
item.description: Mark Toothaker laughed so hard at Younghoe Koo's botched NFL kick that he had a seizure. It may have saved his life.

item.title: Indigenous players defend St Kilda coach Ross Lyon after report reveals comment during training session
item.description: Several indigenous players who have played under Saints coach Ross Lyon come to his defence after comments he made during a training session led to a meeting where Lyon offered to stand down.

item.title: Iran blames US for failed peace talks during visit to Russia — as it happened
item.description: Iran's foreign minister says the US's "excessive demands" were the cause of failed peace talks. Meanwhile, Donald Trump has discussed the latest Iranian proposal. Look back on our live blog.

item.title: Third assassination attempt on Donald Trump highlights US history of political violence
item.description: The latest attack on US President Donald Trump has once again thrust his country's long history of political violence into the spotlight.

item.title: UK parliament's AUKUS inquiry report questions if Britain can keep submarine promises
item.description: The House of Commons Defence Committee releases the findings of its yearlong review into the trilateral defence partnership.

item.title: Donald Trump calls for broadcaster to fire Jimmy Kimmel
item.description: Donald Trump says Jimmy Kimmel should be fired for a monologue he delivered days before the third alleged attempt to assassinate the US president.

item.title: Broken PBS system behind failure to list Mounjaro for type 2 diabetics, according to Medicines Australia
item.description: The "broken" process behind listing medication on the PBS contributes to hundreds of thousands of Australians with diabetes missing out on subsidised lifesaving medication, according to the peak industry body.

item.title: Rock climber dies after falling at Organ Pipes near Mount Arapiles
item.description: The woman was climbing with a group of other climbers at Organ Pipes near Mount Arapiles yesterday.

item.title: Rebel Wilson describes herself as 'champion of women' as actor gives evidence in defamation trial
item.description: The Hollywood star is being sued for defamation in the Federal Court by Charlotte MacInnes, who plays the lead actor in Ms Wilson's debut directorial film The Deb.

item.title: Suspect in Washington dinner shooting charged with trying to assassinate Donald Trump
item.description: The man accused of opening fire at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has been charged with attempting to assassinate the US president and could face life in prison if convicted.

item.title: Former Australian federal commissioner Tom Rogers to lead independent review into SA election
item.description: Former Australian electoral commissioner Tom Rogers is appointed to lead a "sweeping independent review" of the management and oversight of last month's SA state election and First Nations Voice election.

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel faces backlash over Melania Trump 'widow' joke days before shooting
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Canberra Hospital staff raised concerns over secure area before knife incident last week
item.description: No-one was injured during the incident at Canberra Hospital but staff were stuck inside a "secure staff station" for three hours while police disarmed a man with a knife.

item.title: Tasmanian teachers strike three-year deal to eradicate violence and lessen workload in schools
item.description: After months of dispute, an agreement has been reached on Tasmanian teachers' pay and conditions for the next three years.

item.title: CFMEU delegate and former boxer disrupted Cross River Rail site while pretending to be climate activist, inquiry hears
item.description: Queensland's Commission of Inquiry into the CFMEU is this week hearing from contractors on the Cross River Rail project, with revelations former industrial relations minister Grace Grace has retained legal counsel.

item.title: Incat electric ferry waiting in Hobart as heavy-lift transporter is stuck in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The world's largest electric ferry is sitting idle in Tasmania after construction was completed because the heavy-lift ship that was booked to take it to South America is stuck in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: What I've learnt about living on other mobs' Country — respectfully
item.description: To better understand what respect looks like in practice, I spoke with other First Nations people about how they approach working and living on other mobs' Countries.

item.title: Foreign tourist drowns near Yallingup after being swept from rocks at Indijup Spa
item.description: Police say the man, a tourist visiting from South-East Asia with his family, was swept from the rocks at Indijup Natural Spa near Yallingup on Monday.

item.title: Dolphin spotted in Yarra River
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Dolphin spotted in Melbourne's Yarra River probably hunting prey, researcher says
item.description: Researchers say the dolphin appears healthy and is most likely chasing prey in the Melbourne waterway.

item.title: Single-use plastic soy sauce fish are an eco-nightmare. A local designer has a solution
item.description: Shoyu-tai, the single-use plastic soy sauce fish that accompany takeaway sushi, are an eco-nightmare — but a local designer has a solution.

item.title: Coalition points to next-generation stealth bombers as potential AUKUS stopgap
item.description: The Coalition says Australia should consider buying next-generation stealth bombers to help bridge the gap until the AUKUS submarines arrive.

item.title: Weapons maker Thales loses out on multi-billion-dollar StrikeMaster missile deal after Bushmaster win
item.description: Thales was the favourite to win the contract to develop a mobile missile system for Australia, but now the future of its StrikeMaster missile launcher is uncertain.

item.title: Nedra Talley Ross, the last surviving member of the 1960s pop trio The Ronettes, dies aged 80
item.description: Nedra Talley Ross, the last surviving member of the 1960s pop band the Ronettes, who sang the enduring hits Be My Baby, Baby I Love You, and Walking in the Rain alongside her cousins, has died aged 80.

item.title: The NSW public transport Opal network is set to change. Here's what we know
item.description: Digital cards, fresh Opal readers, real-time information and the end of "phantom buses" are being flagged as part of a planned $820 million upgrade to the New South Wales public transport network.

item.title: Millie Elliott returns after having a baby to line up for NSW in State of Origin Game I
item.description: Millie Elliott could have taken an easy route back into competitive action after having a baby. Instead, she is throwing herself straight back into the State of Origin cauldron on Thursday night.

item.title: Why NSW hasn’t met a problem Tiana Penitani Gray couldn’t solve
item.description: Tiana Penitani Gray has never played second row before, but as the Origin stalwart prepares to play her third position in as many series such a bold change is nothing new.

item.title: Global military spending hits record high amid warnings of new arms race
item.description: The world is spending more money on weapons than ever before, reaching nearly $4 trillion in 2025.

item.title: Health insurers accused of 'aggressive' and 'almost bullying' tactics in private hospital dealings
item.description: A former Bupa executive accuses health insurers of using "almost bullying" tactics in their negotiations with private hospitals, while the Australian Private Hospitals Association says insurers have become "cowboys" in need of regulation.

item.title: Donald Trump names former Republican congressman as new US ambassador to Australia
item.description: The Trump administration has nominated former congressman David Brat as ambassador to Australia.

item.title: Alex Johnston, NRL's greatest ever try-scorer, hints at imminent PNG switch from South Sydney
item.description: Record-setting South Sydney try-scoring winger Alex Johnston looks set to become the first player to sign for the PNG Chiefs ahead of their 2028 NRL entry.

item.title: Mother 'always thinks' about whether birth delay at regional hospital led to son's ongoing health issues
item.description: A regional South Australian mother who faced a range of stressful diversions during her son's birth says she will always wonder whether his serious and ongoing health concerns were caused by delays in receiving care.

item.title: Locusts seen on Riverland farm
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Farmers and authorities on alert as Australian plague locust migrates to South Australia
item.description: Multiple locust swarms in South Australia's Riverland region have put farmers and authorities on alert, prompting the South Australian Plague Locust Commissioner to send surveying teams to the region.

item.title: Locust swarms in the Murtho area
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Rockhampton's Plaza Hotel set to go under the hammer, but no inspections allowed
item.description: Rockhampton's Plaza Hotel is set to go under the hammer after closing down more than a decade ago.

item.title: Port Arthur survivor Jane Scholefield delivered a speech on the 30th anniversary of the tragedy
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Memorial service held to remember and reflect on Port Arthur massacre's enduring legacy, 30 years on
item.description: Thirty years on from the Port Arthur tragedy, survivor Jane Scholefield had a simple message for those still bearing the scars of that day — keep love at the centre of mourning.

item.title: New police operation investigating crime syndicates 'pulling the strings' in Melbourne firebombings
item.description: Police are investigating whether recent arson attacks across 15 Melbourne hospitality venues are linked to an organised crime syndicate.

item.title: Police honour Kalgoorlie detectives brutally killed 100 years ago as historians search for murder scene
item.description: On this day in 1926, Detective Inspector John Walsh and Detective Sergeant Alexander Pitman were shot while trying to apprehend gold thieves near Kalgoorlie. Their charred and dismembered bodies were later found at the bottom of a mine shaft.
